paper_record.title: A $Γ$-valley Moiré Platform for Tunable Square Lattice Hubbard Model
paper_record.abstract: Moiré superlattices have emerged as a premier platform for simulating the Hubbard model, yet achieving high tunability in square-lattice systems remains a key challenge. We demonstrate that $Γ$-valley twisted square homobilayers provide a faithful and highly tunable realization of $t-t'-U$ Hubbard model, extending the recent proposal in M-valley systems. We show that at small twist angles, an emergent layer-exchange symmetry decouples electronic states into flat bands residing on two nested square sublattices. An interlayer displacement field breaks this symmetry to induce controllable inter-sublattice hybridization, enabling wide-range experimental tuning of the effective hopping ratio $t'/t$. By establishing a direct correspondence between $Γ$- and M-valley systems, we provide a unified framework for understanding displacement-field tunability in square moiré physics. These findings establish $Γ$-valley twisted bilayers as a versatile platform for simulating the square-lattice Hubbard model and exploring its rich landscape of correlated phenomena.

one_sentence_summary.zh: 本文提出利用Gamma谷扭转方格子同质双层膜构建高度可调的方格子Hubbard模型。
one_sentence_summary.en: This paper proposes Gamma-valley twisted square homobilayers as a highly tunable platform for simulating the square-lattice Hubbard model.

problem.zh: 在方格子莫尔系统中实现高可调性的Hubbard模型（特别是跳跃项比例t'/t）具有挑战性。
problem.en: Achieving high tunability of the hopping ratio t'/t in square-lattice moire Hubbard models remains a significant challenge.

approach.zh: 利用Gamma谷扭转双层膜中的层交换对称性破缺，通过位移场诱导子格点杂化，从而调节有效跳跃参数。
approach.en: Utilizing layer-exchange symmetry breaking in Gamma-valley twisted bilayers to induce controllable inter-sublattice hybridization via a displacement field.

main_takeaway.zh: 位移场可以使t'/t在很大范围内（0到-1.1）连续调节，涵盖了铜氧化物超导体的特征值。
main_takeaway.en: The displacement field enables continuous tuning of the t'/t ratio from 0 to -1.1, covering the regime relevant to cuprate superconductors.

why_it_matters.zh: 该平台为研究t'对d波超导、磁性及量子自旋液体等强关联现象的影响提供了理想场所。
why_it_matters.en: This platform provides an ideal setting to investigate the influence of t' on d-wave superconductivity, magnetism, and quantum spin liquids.

reason.zh: 提出了具有广泛应用前景的新型莫尔物理平台，且建立了统一的理论框架。
reason.en: Proposes a versatile moire platform with a unified theoretical framework and significant implications for correlated physics.
